motion: Merge PR #30: epic: Toolbar, Column Management & Export -- ROUND 2 post-fix audit (commit 5a9e382 applied round-1 fixes D1, D2, D3, C6)

D2.note: aria-pressed added at ColumnDropdown.svelte:50. Round-1 D2 cited role="checkbox"/aria-checked which were never present in any commit; fix is a valid improvement regardless.

C6.note: try/catch around localStorage.setItem added at App.svelte:203-210

findings.description: role="dialog" declared on ColumnDropdown outer container without any of the three mandatory dialog keyboard behaviors (WAI-ARIA 1.2 § 6.6): no focus-on-open (no tick()+.focus() call when columnsOpen becomes true), no Tab containment (no focus trap), and no focus-return-on-close (Escape and outside-click both set columnsOpen=false without restoring focus to the trigger). Toolbar.svelte:129 declares aria-haspopup="dialog" making an AT promise the implementation does not keep.
findings.fix_description: Replace role="dialog" with no role (Disclosure pattern) on ColumnDropdown.svelte:32, and change aria-haspopup="dialog" to aria-haspopup="true" on Toolbar.svelte:129. The column panel is a non-modal disclosure widget -- users can Tab out naturally, the trigger already carries aria-expanded, and WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure pattern has no focus-trap requirement. ~4 lines total across two files. Alternative (not recommended): keep role="dialog" and implement all three behaviors (~15-20 lines).

findings.description: Dead `panel` binding left behind by the fix commit (5a9e382). `let panel: HTMLDivElement | null = null;` is declared at ColumnDropdown.svelte:17 and bound via bind:this at line 32, but nothing reads it -- the onMount block that would have consumed it was removed by the fix commit. Grep confirms no read access to `panel` anywhere in the script block.
findings.fix_description: Remove `let panel: HTMLDivElement | null = null;` declaration at line 17 and remove `bind:this={panel}` from the outer div at line 32. ~2 lines removed, no behavior change.

concessions.point: D2 phantom: round-1 cited role="checkbox" and aria-checked which were never present in any commit of ColumnDropdown.svelte. aria-pressed addition is a valid improvement regardless.
R2-1: role="dialog" without focus management is a real ARIA defect introduced by the PR and missed by round 1.
R2-2: dead `panel` binding is real dead code.
BLOCKING label withdrawn; CONDITIONAL is the correct treatment given the surgical fix surface (~4 lines total).

conditions: Apply Fix R2-1 on this branch before merging.
Apply Fix R2-2 on this branch before merging.
Verify in a screen reader or Lighthouse accessibility audit that the ColumnDropdown announces as a region/disclosure (not a dialog) and no 'dialog opened but focus did not move' error is reported.
summary: Round 2 post-fix audit verified that all 4 round-1 fixes (D1, D2, D3, C6) are correctly applied in commit 5a9e382, and the PR's core features (debounce coordination, race guard, localStorage resilience, spec Must-Haves) remain sound and untouched. Two new defects surfaced that round 1 missed: (1) role="dialog" on ColumnDropdown is declared with none of the three WAI-ARIA 1.2 § 6.6 mandatory dialog keyboard behaviors, and (2) the panel variable is dead code left behind by the fix commit. Both debaters converged quickly on CONDITIONAL with surgical fixes (~4 lines total across two files). Also on the record: round-1 D2 cited role="checkbox" code that was never present -- a phantom defect -- though the aria-pressed improvement it produced is still correct and stands.
